mappings.rationale: Directly covers the nature and methods of historical writing, the central concern of this section on historiography and the study of history.
Represents a specific type of historical writing (section A.2), illustrating how historians approach intellectual and political history.
Section B.2.b lists archaeology as an auxiliary discipline for ascertaining and interpreting historical sources.
Herodotus is the 'father of history' and central to section A.1.a on the development of historiography in the ancient world.
Thucydides is a foundational figure in historiography, central to section A.1.a on historical writing in the ancient world.
Section B.2.a lists anthropology as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Section B.2.i lists geography as an auxiliary discipline for historical research and interpreting sources.
Section B.2.l lists linguistics as an auxiliary discipline for ascertaining and interpreting historical sources.
Classical studies involve historiography of the ancient world (A.1.a) and employ many of the auxiliary disciplines listed in section B.2.
Section B.2.g lists epigraphy as an auxiliary discipline, and hieroglyphics are a primary form of ancient written source material.
Represents a specific approach to historical writing (section A.2), demonstrating diverse types of historiographic method and purpose.
Represents a specific type of historical writing (section A.2), illustrating social history as a methodological approach.
Represents a specific historiographic approach (section A.2), illustrating diverse ways of classifying and writing history.
Represents intellectual history as a type of historical writing (section A.2) with specific methods and concerns.
Cultural history represents a type of historical writing (section A.2) with distinctive methods and sources.
Economic history is a type of historical writing (section A.2) employing quantitative methods and economic analysis.
Prehistory deals with material remains (B.1) and relies on auxiliary disciplines like archaeology (B.2.b) and dendrochronology (B.2.e).
Biblical archaeology combines archaeology (B.2.b) with textual criticism (B.2.q) as auxiliary historical disciplines.
Forensic methods relate to the auxiliary disciplines for interpreting historical sources, including material remains (B.1).
Biography represents a specific type of historical writing (section A.2) with its own methods and conventions.
Autobiography is both a historical source (B.1) and a type of historical writing (A.2) involving the author's vantage point (A.3).
Dictionaries serve as historical sources (B.1) and connect to linguistics (B.2.l) as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.
Section B.2.o lists radiometric dating as an auxiliary discipline for historical research, which relies on radioactivity.
Section C covers philosophy of history including speculations about the historical process and philosophical analysis of historical writing.
Oral history is a methodology for historical research involving interviews, archives, and narrative — directly relevant to this section on historiography and the study of history.
